mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in less than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing equity risk premiums to widen and S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 6.0% threshold, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer spending and residential investment, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of co-occurring positive deviations, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation above 230, the Federal Reserve maintains restrictive policy, causing sudden negative repricing of equity valuations when inflation data confirms persistent price pressures, leading to negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation observations, or the signal reverses direction during confirmed disinflation phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and stronger business investment, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year window, or positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative equity returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 150,000 signal robust labor demand and sustained wage growth, which supports consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where payroll growth surpasses the 150,000 threshold, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflationary pressures erode corporate profit margins and force monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of slowing GDPC1 and high CPIAUCSL yields positive forward returns in more than 55% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market regime where unemployment stays below 5.0%, rising industrial production indicates supply-side resilience without triggering wage-driven inflation, allowing equities to reprice higher on earnings optimism over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns turn negative in more than 65% of instances when INDPRO rises under the specified unemployment threshold, or the signal shows no edge in regime-filtered backtests.
